expectations.text: Identifies that reference-depth/advanced material (e.g. conditional-filter boolean gates, optional-filter substitution semantics, schema-gate error codes, operation timing) exceeds getting-started altitude or needs moving/condensing
expectations.evidence: Altitude section: "Schema Gate (335-378) names specific server error codes ... reference-manual depth"; Structure #2 "Conditional Filters (Boolean Gates) is too deep for this page"; #3 "Operation Timing ... is a debugging/reference detail."

expectations.text: Makes an ordering/structure point: advanced material should sit below (not between/above) introductory and common-path content, or the page should be restructured along those lines
expectations.evidence: Structure #1: "Flat, undifferentiated section list ... no grouping into 'core path' vs 'going further' ... either (a) splitting the advanced half into a separate page, or (b) adding a 'Beyond the basics' divider ... and demoting the deep sections beneath it."

expectations.text: Flags the '(SQLite-backed)' implementation-internal mention in the intro
expectations.evidence: Defect #3: "'SQLite-backed' (line 3) leaks the storage engine. Per the repo's standard of describing developer-facing impact rather than backend internals, 'SQLite-backed' should be dropped."

expectations.text: Proposes concrete dispositions (condense, move to reference/agent docs, reorder) rather than only describing problems
expectations.evidence: Recommended actions: "Add a 'core vs. advanced' boundary. Insert a divider after 'Access Control with CEL' and move (or relocate to a reference/patterns page) Conditional Filter Gates, Optional Filter Fields ... Schema Gate error codes, Operation Timing ..."

claims.claim: The fixture is 676 lines, the longest getting-started page, vs 546 in the live shipped page
claims.evidence: Calibration table compares peer-page line counts; the fixture working-with-databases.md is plausibly the longest. Page-length comparison is a reasonable altitude signal.

claims.claim: The db-execute-operation code-group is duplicated (lines 95-97 and 124-131)
claims.evidence: Defect #1 cites specific line ranges; a concrete redundancy claim consistent with a verbose fixture variant.

claims.claim: 'raw SQL' (line 16) is self-contradicting given the schemaless framing
claims.evidence: Defect #4 reasoning is sound — the store is schemaless JSON, so 'no raw SQL' implies a SQL surface; defensible editorial point.

eval_feedback.overall: No suggestions, evals look solid. This run hit all four assertions, including the easy-to-miss SQLite-backed leak, and added well-grounded extra findings (duplicated code-group, triple-stated limit/cursor note).
